svg.text: McCzarny recently updated his personal
repository's README to showcase recent GitHub
activity with new layout and contribution
shields for Qt and Chromium. In
chromium-daily-digest, he addressed issues with
transforming overview links and adjusted job
timing. A significant portion of his activity
was in crater-js, where he introduced stamina
mechanics for character abilities and refined
inventory and character movement. He enhanced
the game's UI by adding tooltips to character
portraits, action indicators, and enabling
character selection. McCzarny also added new
game features like ladder functionality, a
trading UI, item dropping, and cave generation.
He introduced a new mob type, the Essence
Spider, and implemented various visual updates,
including new textures, icons, and HUD
elements. Key mechanics like falling boulders,
resource drops, stamina for digging, and
diagonal digging were also added or refined.
The crater-js project saw a migration to
TypeScript and improvements in overall game
logic and character actions. For
flexible-log-analyzer, he updated to a newer VS
Code version and performed a major dependency
bump across 9 packages, including eslint and
typescript. Finally, McCzarny also pushed
dependency updates to his upload-image action.
